scenes.text: On the Seoul Metro and not sure where to switch lines. Ask a fellow passenger or station staff!
The Seoul subway has 9+ lines and goes everywhere — transfers are common.
Tip: Naver Maps and Kakao Maps are better than Google Maps for Seoul navigation!

sections.insight: Korean question words: 어디 (where), 뭐 (what), 언제 (when), 누구 (who), 왜 (why), 어떻게 (how). Unlike English, they stay in their natural sentence position — the sentence just goes up in tone at the end to signal a question!

sections.stepTitle: Say where you want to go!
sections.instruction: "___e gago sipeoyo" = "I want to go to ___". Pick a destination! 👇

questions.question: How do you ask "Where do I transfer?"
questions.correctMsg: Correct! 어디서 갈아타요? — 어디 = where, 갈아타다 = to transfer. Natural and clear!
questions.wrongMsg: Not quite! The answer is 어디서 갈아타요? (eo-di-seo ga-ra-ta-yo?) — 갈아타다 specifically means 'to transfer between vehicles'.

questions.question: How do you say "I want to go to Gyeongbokgung"?
questions.sub: Think back to the pattern practice!
questions.correctMsg: Perfect! 경복궁에 가고 싶어요 — the -에 particle marks direction (to/at). Swap any destination!
questions.wrongMsg: Not quite! 경복궁에 가고 싶어요 — 가고 싶어요 = I want to go. -에 = to (direction).

sections.stepTitle: Seoul's subway is one of the best in the world

culture.title: The Seoul Metro: clean, fast, and everywhere
culture.body:
The Seoul subway has 9 main lines + multiple branches, covers the entire city, and runs until midnight. It's cheap (about ₩1,400 per ride), air-conditioned, and has English signs at every station.

Essential tips:
• Get a T-money card (티머니) from any convenience store — tap in and out for discounts
• Line 2 (green) is the most-used line, circling the city
• Quiet cars exist — no calls, no loud music
• Phone signal works everywhere underground!

drama.note: These come up constantly in K-dramas. 조심해 is casual — say 조심하세요 to strangers ✨
